pool_recommendation.explanation: Balanced leverage. Targets a champion with positive value score (win prob > pick share) and seed ≤ 3. The sweet spot of meaningful win probability plus differentiation from the field.
pool_recommendation.strategy_note: Medium pool: one well-differentiated champion beats chasing upsets. Pick a team with positive value (win prob > pick share) — ideally a 1–3 seed the public is under-valuing.
pool_recommendation.primary_reason: Michigan carries a 17.7% title probability against only 14.2% public pick share — 1.2× value, 50.7% Final Four probability. In a 100-person pool this is the sweet spot: meaningful win probability AND differentiation from the field.

deterministic_recommendation.label: Deterministic
deterministic_recommendation.archetype: Pure model output, no pool adjustment
deterministic_recommendation.description: The model's single best prediction of the most likely tournament outcome. No pool-size differentiation applied. Use this as your baseline before layering in strategy.

safe_recommendation.label: Safe
safe_recommendation.description: Low-variance champion pick. Maximizes title probability. Best for shallow pools where being right matters more than differentiation — consensus picks pay well when the field is small.
safe_recommendation.primary_reason: Duke has the highest title probability in the field (22.1%), 57.6% Final Four probability. In a shallow pool, picking the most likely winner maximises your chance of taking the pot outright.

value_recommendation.label: Value
value_recommendation.description: Balanced leverage. Targets a champion with positive value score (win prob > pick share) and seed ≤ 3. The sweet spot of meaningful win probability plus differentiation from the field.
value_recommendation.primary_reason: Michigan carries a 17.7% title probability against only 14.2% public pick share — 1.2× value, 50.7% Final Four probability. In a 60-person pool this is the sweet spot: meaningful win probability AND differentiation from the field.

contrarian_recommendation.label: Contrarian
contrarian_recommendation.description: Maximum differentiation. Targets the highest-leverage champion the public is under-picking. Maximizes expected payout in crowded pools where owning an unpopular champion is the only path to a big win.
contrarian_recommendation.primary_reason: Illinois: 5.2% title probability, 1.4% public pick share (3.64× value, 24.9% Final Four probability). Estimated ~7 other entries in your pool will pick this team — but at 3.6× value, this is the highest-leverage champion available.
